risk_if_not_implemented: Without Customer Responsibility Matrix (CRM), residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Access Enforcement
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to enforce Logical Access Control (LAC) permissions that conform to the principle of "least privilege."
compensating_control_1.justification: Access Enforcement (IAC-20) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Customer Responsibility Matrix (CRM) (CLD-06.1) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to ensure network architecture utilizes network segmentation to isolate Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) to protect from other network resources.
compensating_control_2.justification: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation) (NET-06) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Customer Responsibility Matrix (CRM) (CLD-06.1) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
